message:
fix(packs/database/sqlite): close compound-command bypass via .dump/.schema

Overly broad safe patterns (`\.schema`, `\.tables`, `\.dump`, `\.backup`)
matched those substrings *anywhere* in the command. A compound statement
like:

  sqlite3 mydb "DROP TABLE foo; .dump"
  sqlite3 mydb "DELETE FROM users; .schema"

would match the safe `.dump`/`.schema` pattern, short-circuit the pack,
and never reach the destructive `DROP TABLE` / `DELETE FROM` check.

Replaced with two anchored forms:
- `sqlite3-dot-command`: requires the dot-command to be the quoted SQL
  argument passed to sqlite3
- `dot-command-standalone`: requires the dot-command to be at the very
  start of the command (for bare REPL input)

Regression test verifies the bypass is closed and standalone dot-commands
still pass through as safe.
